_note: Worked example: Meridian Freightways, a fictional asset-light regional freight brokerage / 3PL. Order intake lives in Atlas OMS (customer master), execution in LoadLine TMS (locations, lanes, carriers, shipments), PODs in HaulDoc DMS, carrier settlement in SettleTrack AP, and carrier status flows through the FreightPulse EDI gateway. The shipment lifecycle (booked -> picked_up -> in_transit -> delivered/exception -> pod_received) is a right-censored state machine; carrier invoices, POD documents, exception cases, and chained shipment legs are all DERIVED from machine outcomes so timestamps cohere by construction. Keys starting with '_' are comments and are stripped before validation.

calendar._note: Freight weekday shape: pickups peak Mon-Wed, Saturday trickle, Sunday near zero. Month weights carry the Q4 peak-season ramp (Oct-Dec index ~1.2).

accessorial_note: Driver held at consignee past free time, detention timer attached.
Liftgate required at delivery, no dock available.
Redelivery after first attempt refused at gate.
Layover authorized by dispatch, receiver closed early.
Reweigh ordered at origin scale, certificate on file.
Detention at shipper, loading crew short-staffed.
Charge approved per customer routing guide.
Pallet exchange fee per facility policy.
After-hours delivery surcharge per appointment note.

tables.name: application
tables.purpose: Application catalog for the fictional landscape.

tables._note: Shipper customer master in the OMS. created_at is sorted so customer IDs correlate with onboarding order, and 62% of the book predates the data window so in-window volume grows ~8%/yr instead of ramping from zero. Vertical is drawn first so company-name flavor matches it.
tables.name: customer
tables.purpose: Shipper customer accounts (manufacturers, retailers, food and consumer-goods companies).

columns.name: email
columns._note: Role addresses on the shipper's own fictional subdomain (logistics@<company>.example.com) - all-personal emails on a B2B master is a tell.

tables.name: location
tables.purpose: Network locations: shipper docks, consignee docks, crossdock terminals, DCs, ports.

tables._note: Lanes carry the rating engine: per-lane rate-per-mile draws a sell rate off distance, buy rates hang off the sell rate at ~85% (the broker margin), and LTL/intermodal rate columns are pre-computed so shipments can fk_copy the rate that matches their mode. Lane FK targets are role-filtered with fk-affinity matching (origins from shipper docks/DCs, destinations from consignee docks/DCs) with a small leak.
tables.name: lane
tables.purpose: Origin-destination lanes with contracted sell rates and target buy rates.

tables._note: Contracted carrier base. duplicate_entity later injects DBA-vs-legal-name doubles that core.carrier survivorship collapses (and the mdm_stewards queue works).
tables.name: carrier
tables.purpose: Contracted motor carriers with tier, safety rating and quick-pay terms.

tables._note: TMS bill-to mirror of the OMS customer, generated 1:1 with parent_copy so the two systems share entities coherently; the xref crosswalk then maps both, and post-derivation imperfections make the systems disagree realistically.
tables.name: billto_account
tables.purpose: TMS billing account mirror of the OMS customer master, synced nightly.

tables._note: The operational core. Volume is conditioned on segment (national shippers tender ~8x what SMBs do after the base draw) plus a heavy-tailed per-customer multiplier so a whale tier emerges. Lane and carrier picks are zipf-weighted (top lanes/carriers carry most loads). Rating: sell = contracted lane rate x mode factor (exact repeats, like real contract rates); buy = mode-matched lane buy rate with +/-20% spot jitter, so margin averages ~15% and ~5% of loads ship at a loss. Lifecycle timestamps are stamped by the state machine.
tables.name: shipment
tables.purpose: Shipment headers booked against a customer, lane and carrier.

columns.name: lane_id
columns._note: zipf 1.1: top lane ~18% of loads, top decile of lanes carries most volume, long tail of one-off lanes.

columns.name: carrier_id
columns._note: zipf 1.1 over the carrier base: top 20 carriers haul roughly half the loads.

columns.name: booked_date
columns._note: Calendar-weighted and bounded by the customer's onboarding date - weekday/Q4 shape AND no shipments before the customer existed.

columns.name: fuel_pct
columns._note: Quantized weekly-index values, not a continuous draw - fuel surcharge tables move in steps.

columns.name: sell_linehaul
columns._note: Contract rate x mode factor: exact values repeat per lane+mode, which is what real contracted amount columns look like (duplicate-mass realism check).

columns.name: buy_linehaul
columns._note: Spot-market buy: the mode-matched lane buy quote with +/-20% jitter. Margin = 1 - buy/sell averages ~15%; ~5% of loads go out at a loss.

tables.name: shipment_event
tables.purpose: Shipment milestone history emitted by the lifecycle state machine.
tables.grain: one row per shipment per state entered

tables._note: Accessorials hit ~34% of shipments (poisson 0.42). Amounts are conditioned on charge type and land on whole/half dollars like real tariff tables; detention rows carry a dwell-minutes reading above the 120-minute free time. manual_override later flags ~7% as analyst-added/waived, which DQ-002 catches.
tables.name: accessorial_event
tables.purpose: Accessorial charge events captured against shipments (detention, lumper, liftgate...).
tables.grain: one row per accessorial charge event

tables._note: EDI 214/210/204 traffic per shipment. This is where the integration imperfections live: late-arriving partner statuses (restating OTP), duplicate gateway deliveries (DQ-006), and PRO-unmapped orphans (DQ-007).
tables.name: edi_message
tables.purpose: Inbound EDI messages from carriers and trading partners.
tables.grain: one row per received EDI message

tables._note: Generator-fed ops queues for everything the imperfections produce downstream: carrier invoice mismatches, missing PODs, unmapped EDI, accessorial disputes, duplicate carriers. Runs through the case lifecycle machine (right-censored: recent items legitimately sit open/triaged).
tables.name: work_item
tables.purpose: Operational work queues (settlement desk, billing ops, integration support, MDM stewards).

tables.name: work_item_event
tables.purpose: Work item status history from the case lifecycle machine.
tables.grain: one row per work item per state entered

tables.name: rule
tables.purpose: Data quality rule catalog (results come from the dq_rule_result_current view).

cases.1: carrier invoice PRO matches a shipment
cases.7: EDI message maps to a shipment
cases.8: xref mapping not expired while active

tables.name: recon_control
tables.purpose: Reconciliation control catalog; open breaks come from the control_recon_shipment_carrier_invoice view.

cases.1: delivered shipments reconcile to carrier invoices
cases.2: carrier invoice header equals line sum
cases.3: POD on file within 5 days of delivery

tables._note: Everything below is populated by SQL derivations - real lineage, not parallel-faked data.
tables.name: oms_customer_extract
tables.purpose: Nightly OMS customer extract as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables.name: oms_customer
tables.purpose: Cleaned OMS customers; normalizes raw extract dates (drift rows parse to NULL and feed DQ-004).

tables.name: entity_identifier
tables.purpose: Source-system identifier crosswalk for canonical customers (Atlas OMS + LoadLine TMS).

tables.name: customer
tables.purpose: Canonical customer joining staged OMS data to the TMS billto through the crosswalk.

tables.name: carrier
tables.purpose: Canonical carrier: survivorship collapses duplicate-onboarded carriers to the oldest record per SCAC.

tables._note: Legs are DERIVED from machine outcomes: leg counts follow the 0.55/0.30/0.10/0.05 industry split (intermodal forced multi-leg), intermediate stops chain through real crossdock terminals (leg n dest = leg n+1 origin), and the picked_up->delivered window is split into travel segments separated by lognormal-shaped terminal dwells. In-flight shipments get projected ETAs (planned/in_transit legs).
tables.name: shipment_leg
tables.purpose: Executed/planned legs per shipment, chained through crossdock terminals.
tables.grain: one row per shipment per leg sequence

tables.name: pod_document
tables.purpose: Proof-of-delivery document metadata, one per shipment with a received POD; capture method correlates with POD lag (fast PODs come from the driver app).
tables.grain: one row per received POD document

tables.name: exception_case
tables.purpose: Exception cases opened 1:1 from shipments that hit the exception state; status and resolution timestamps cohere with the shipment outcome (recovered -> resolved, returned -> closed, in-flight -> investigating/escalated).

tables._note: Carrier invoices derive from delivered shipments with a 3-25 day submission lag (right-censored: recent deliveries are legitimately uninvoiced). ~27% carry a deliberate rate variance and route to exception_review, ~1.2% of shipments are never billed (the missing-invoice recon slice). PRO typos and line restatements are injected post-derivation.
tables.name: carrier_invoice
tables.purpose: Carrier freight invoices received for delivered shipments.
tables.grain: one row per carrier invoice (1:1 with billed shipment)

tables.name: carrier_invoice_line
tables.purpose: Carrier invoice charge lines: linehaul (with rate variance vs rated), fuel surcharge, accessorial passthroughs.
tables.grain: one row per carrier invoice charge line

tables.name: dim_date
tables.purpose: Calendar dimension.

tables.name: dim_customer
tables.purpose: Customer dimension from the canonical layer.

tables.name: dim_carrier
tables.purpose: Carrier dimension from the canonical (deduped) layer.

tables.name: dim_lane
tables.purpose: Lane dimension with origin/destination geography.

tables.name: fact_shipment
tables.purpose: Shipment fact with revenue/cost/margin economics, OTP flags, exception flag and POD lag.

tables.name: fact_carrier_settlement_line
tables.purpose: Carrier settlement line fact as loaded to the warehouse (nightly snapshot: post-load line restatements deliberately lag here and surface in the recon view first).
tables.grain: one row per carrier invoice line

state_machines._note: Right-censored shipment lifecycle. Residual probabilities leave open pipeline (stuck bookings, in-transit loads, unresolved exceptions, delivered-no-POD). Exception recovery dwell (3.5d) makes exception shipments mostly late, which is what drags OTP from ~88% to ~84%.
state_machines.name: shipment_lifecycle

derivations.name: raw_oms_customer_extract
derivations._note: Raw landing copy with US-format dates; format_drift later pads a slice of these, which the staging parse turns into NULLs that DQ-004 catches.
sql: select cast(customer_id as text), customer_code, customer_name, segment, industry_vertical, region, city, state, status,

derivations.name: stg_oms_customer
sql: select cast(customer_id as integer), customer_code, trim(customer_name), lower(segment), industry_vertical, region, city, state, lower(status),

derivations.name: xref_entity_identifier
derivations._note: A crosswalk that maps a single system is not a crosswalk - both OMS and TMS identifiers are mapped. missing_xref/stale_mapping imperfections hit this table post-derivation.
sql: select row_number() over (order by c.customer_id), 'customer', c.customer_id,
'Atlas OMS', 'customer', cast(c.customer_id as text), date(c.created_at), null, 1
select 100000 + row_number() over (order by b.billto_id), 'customer', b.oms_customer_id,
'LoadLine TMS', 'billto_account', cast(b.billto_id as text), date(b.created_at), null, 1

derivations.name: core_customer
sql: select s.customer_id, s.customer_name, s.segment, s.industry_vertical, s.region, s.city, s.state, s.status,
(select cast(x.source_record_id as integer) from xref.entity_identifier x
where x.canonical_entity_id = s.customer_id and x.source_system = 'LoadLine TMS'

derivations.name: core_carrier
derivations._note: Survivorship: the duplicate-onboarded carriers injected pre-derivation share a SCAC, so min(carrier_id) per SCAC collapses them to the original record.
sql: select c.carrier_id, c.carrier_code, c.carrier_name, c.carrier_tier, c.safety_rating, c.quick_pay_flag
where c.carrier_id = (select min(c2.carrier_id) from tms.carrier c2 where c2.carrier_code = c.carrier_code);

derivations.name: tms_shipment_leg
derivations._note: Chained legs: leg counts from a deterministic 0.55/0.30/0.10/0.05 split (intermodal forced multi-leg); intermediate stops walk real terminals so leg n dest = leg n+1 origin; the picked_up->delivered window is split into equal travel segments separated by terminal dwells drawn from 16 lognormal(median 9h, p90 ~28h) quantile values, squeezed to at most 55% of the window so travel time never goes negative. Undelivered shipments use the planned-transit ETA and get planned/in_transit leg statuses.
sql: select location_id, row_number() over (order by location_id) - 1 as rk
tc as (select count(*) as cnt from term),
select s.shipment_id, s.carrier_id, s.origin_location_id, s.dest_location_id, s.distance_miles,
+ (case when s.mode = 'intermodal' and (s.shipment_id * 13) % 20 < 11 then 1 else 0 end)) as n_legs,
seqs as (select 1 as n union all select 2 union all select 3 union all select 4),
when 0 then 1.9 when 1 then 2.9 when 2 then 3.8 when 3 then 4.7 when 4 then 5.5 when 5 then 6.4
when 6 then 7.4 when 7 then 8.4 when 8 then 9.6 when 9 then 11.0 when 10 then 12.7 when 11 then 14.7
when 12 then 17.4 when 13 then 21.2 when 14 then 27.6 else 43.8 end
from base b join seqs q on q.n <= b.n_legs
sum(dwell_raw) over (partition by shipment_id) as dwell_tot,
coalesce(sum(dwell_raw) over (partition by shipment_id order by leg_seq
rows between unbounded preceding and 1 preceding), 0) as dwell_before
case when a.dwell_tot > 0.55 * a.window_hours and a.dwell_tot > 0
then (0.55 * a.window_hours) / a.dwell_tot else 1.0 end as squeeze,
select row_number() over (order by c.shipment_id, c.leg_seq) as leg_id,
case when c.leg_seq = 1 then c.origin_location_id else t1.location_id end,
case when c.leg_seq = c.n_legs then c.dest_location_id else t2.location_id end,
datetime(c.picked_up_at, '+' || cast(round(((c.leg_seq - 1) * c.travel_leg_hours + c.dwell_before * c.squeeze) * 60.0) as integer) || ' minutes'),
case when c.leg_seq = c.n_legs and c.delivered_at is not null then c.delivered_at
else datetime(c.picked_up_at, '+' || cast(round((c.leg_seq * c.travel_leg_hours + c.dwell_before * c.squeeze) * 60.0) as integer) || ' minutes') end,
case when c.leg_seq < c.n_legs then round(c.dwell_raw * c.squeeze, 2) else 0.0 end,
case when c.delivered_at is not null then 'completed'
when datetime(c.picked_up_at, '+' || cast(round(((c.leg_seq - 1) * c.travel_leg_hours + c.dwell_before * c.squeeze) * 60.0) as integer) || ' minutes') > '2025-12-31 23:59:59' then 'planned'
left join term t1 on c.leg_seq > 1 and t1.rk = (c.shipment_id * 3 + (c.leg_seq - 1)) % tc.cnt
left join term t2 on c.leg_seq < c.n_legs and t2.rk = (c.shipment_id * 3 + c.leg_seq) % tc.cnt;

derivations.name: dms_pod_document
derivations._note: POD lag itself comes from the state machine's delivered->pod_received dwell (lognormal median 9h, p99 ~ a week). Capture method is derived FROM the lag, so driver-app PODs are fast and email scans are the slow tail - a correlation reviewers actually check.
sql: case s.shipment_id % 11 when 0 then 'R. Okafor' when 1 then 'M. Delgado' when 2 then 'S. Winters'
when 3 then 'T. Brannigan' when 4 then 'L. Fontaine' when 5 then 'D. Kowalski' when 6 then 'J. Mercado'
when 7 then 'A. Hollis' when 8 then 'K. Vance' when 9 then 'P. Okonkwo' else 'G. Marchetti' end,
case when (s.shipment_id * 3) % 20 < 14 then 'clear' when (s.shipment_id * 3) % 20 < 18 then 'fair' else 'poor' end,
1 + (case when s.shipment_id % 4 = 0 then 1 else 0 end) + (case when s.shipment_id % 9 = 0 then 1 else 0 end),

derivations.name: ops_exception_case
derivations._note: One case per shipment that entered the exception state; customs_hold reasons only occur on cross-border lanes; case status follows the shipment outcome so status-vs-timestamp coherence holds.
sql: case when s.cross_border_flag = 1 and s.shipment_id % 5 = 0 then 'customs_hold'
case when s.delivered_at is not null and s.shipment_id % 17 = 0 then 'closed_with_credit'
when s.delivered_at is not null then 'resolved'
when s.returned_at is not null then 'closed_returned'
case s.shipment_id % 6 when 0 then 'dana.whitfield' when 1 then 'marcus.webb' when 2 then 'priya.raman'
when 3 then 'jordan.beck' when 4 then 'elena.vargas' else 'tomas.keller' end,
case when s.delivered_at is not null then min(datetime(s.delivered_at, '+' || ((s.shipment_id * 13) % 360 + 30) || ' minutes'), '2025-12-31 23:59:59')
when s.returned_at is not null then min(datetime(s.returned_at, '+' || ((s.shipment_id * 13) % 360 + 30) || ' minutes'), '2025-12-31 23:59:59')

derivations.name: settlement_carrier_invoice
derivations._note: Submission lag = 16 lognormal(median 9d, max 25d) quantile values keyed off shipment_id; invoices that would land after the as-of date do not exist yet (right-censoring). The variance multiplier v drives match_status: exact -> auto_matched (~72%), off-rate -> exception_review. shipment_id % 83 = 0 loads are never billed - the deliberate missing-invoice recon slice.
sql: select s.shipment_id, s.carrier_id, s.pro_number, s.buy_linehaul, s.fuel_pct, s.delivered_at,
when 0 then 3 when 1 then 4 when 2 then 5 when 3 then 6 when 4 then 7 when 5 then 7
when 6 then 8 when 7 then 9 when 8 then 9 when 9 then 10 when 10 then 11 when 11 then 12
when 12 then 14 when 13 then 16 when 14 then 19 else 25 end) || ' days') as invoice_date,
where s.delivered_at is not null and s.shipment_id % 83 <> 0
acc as (select shipment_id, round(sum(charge_amount), 2) as amt from tms.accessorial_event group by shipment_id),
select b.*, coalesce(a.amt, 0) as acc_amt, c.quick_pay_flag,
left join acc a on a.shipment_id = b.shipment_id
join tms.carrier c on c.carrier_id = b.carrier_id
case when e.v = 1.0 then 'auto_matched' else 'exception_review' end,
case when e.v <> 1.0 and e.age_days > 45 then 'paid'
or (e.v = 1.0 and ((e.quick_pay_flag = 1 and e.age_days > 7) or (e.quick_pay_flag = 0 and e.age_days > 32)))
then date(e.invoice_date, '+' || (case when e.quick_pay_flag = 1 then 7 else 30 end) || ' days')

derivations.name: settlement_carrier_invoice_line
derivations._note: Linehaul line re-derives the same deterministic variance multiplier so header total = line sum exactly (until restatement pairs are injected post-derivation, which is what makes CTL-002 break). Accessorial passthroughs carry the event amount.
sql: from settlement.carrier_invoice i join tms.shipment s on s.shipment_id = i.shipment_id;
from settlement.carrier_invoice i join tms.shipment s on s.shipment_id = i.shipment_id;
row_number() over (partition by shipment_id order by accessorial_id) as rn
from tms.accessorial_event) a on a.shipment_id = i.shipment_id;

derivations.name: wh_dim_date
sql: union all select date(dt, '+1 day') from d where dt < '2025-12-31'
select dt, cast(strftime('%Y', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%m', dt) as integer),
cast(strftime('%d', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%w', dt) as integer),
case when strftime('%w', dt) in ('0', '6') then 1 else 0 end

derivations.name: wh_dim_customer
sql: select customer_uid, customer_name, segment, industry_vertical, region, status from core.customer;

derivations.name: wh_dim_carrier
sql: select carrier_uid, carrier_name, carrier_tier, safety_rating, quick_pay_flag from core.carrier;

derivations.name: wh_dim_lane
sql: select l.lane_id, l.lane_code, o.city, o.state, d.city, d.state, l.distance_miles, l.cross_border_flag
join tms.location o on o.location_id = l.origin_location_id
join tms.location d on d.location_id = l.dest_location_id;

derivations.name: wh_fact_shipment
derivations._note: On-time delivery = delivered on/before pickup appointment + planned transit days (the certified appointment-based definition). Customer-side accessorial billing carries an 18% margin over the carrier passthrough.
sql: with acc as (select shipment_id, count(*) as n_acc, round(sum(charge_amount), 2) as amt
lg as (select shipment_id, count(*) as n from tms.shipment_leg group by shipment_id),
select s.*, coalesce(a.amt, 0) as acc_amt, coalesce(a.n_acc, 0) as n_acc, coalesce(l.n, 0) as n_legs,
left join acc a on a.shipment_id = s.shipment_id
left join lg l on l.shipment_id = s.shipment_id
select shipment_id, shipment_number, booked_date, customer_id, carrier_id, lane_id, mode, service_level, status,
round(sell_linehaul + fuel_rev + acc_rev - buy_linehaul - fuel_cost - acc_amt, 2),
round((sell_linehaul + fuel_rev + acc_rev - buy_linehaul - fuel_cost - acc_amt)
case when delivered_at is not null then round(julianday(delivered_at) - julianday(picked_up_at), 2) end,
case when picked_up_at is null then null
when date(picked_up_at) <= pickup_appointment_date then 1 else 0 end,
case when delivered_at is null then null
when date(delivered_at) <= date(pickup_appointment_date, '+' || planned_transit_days || ' days') then 1 else 0 end,
case when delivered_at is not null then 1 else 0 end,
case when exception_at is not null then 1 else 0 end,
case when pod_received_at is not null then round((julianday(pod_received_at) - julianday(delivered_at)) * 24, 1) end,

derivations.name: wh_fact_carrier_settlement_line
sql: select l.line_id, l.carrier_invoice_id, i.shipment_id, i.carrier_id, i.invoice_date,
l.charge_type, l.billed_amount, l.rated_amount, round(l.billed_amount - l.rated_amount, 2), i.match_status
join settlement.carrier_invoice i on i.carrier_invoice_id = l.carrier_invoice_id;

derivations.name: mart_on_time_performance
derivations._note: The on-time-performance mart: monthly OTP by carrier tier with exception rates. A view, so it reflects post-imperfection data.
sql: select strftime('%Y-%m', f.booked_date) as month_key, c.carrier_tier,
round(1.0 * sum(coalesce(f.on_time_pickup_flag, 0)) / nullif(sum(case when f.on_time_pickup_flag is not null then 1 else 0 end), 0), 4) as pickup_otp_pct,
join wh.dim_carrier c on c.carrier_key = f.carrier_key

derivations.name: mart_carrier_scorecard
sql: join wh.dim_carrier c on c.carrier_key = f.carrier_key
round(1.0 * sum(case when match_status = 'exception_review' then 1 else 0 end) / count(*), 4) as invoice_exception_rate
from settlement.carrier_invoice group by carrier_id) i on i.carrier_id = c.carrier_key
group by c.carrier_key, c.carrier_name, c.carrier_tier, i.invoice_exception_rate

derivations.name: control_recon_shipment_carrier_invoice
derivations._note: Reconciliation as a VIEW so it reflects post-derivation imperfections. Breaks: never-billed delivered loads (the %83 slice, after the 35-day submission window), header-vs-line mismatches (restatement pairs), and billed-vs-rated variance beyond 2%/10-unit tolerance (the deliberate off-rate slice plus larger restatements).
sql: select s.shipment_id, s.shipment_number, date(s.delivered_at) as delivered_on,
round(s.buy_linehaul + round(s.buy_linehaul * s.fuel_pct, 2) + coalesce(a.amt, 0), 2) as rated_cost
left join (select shipment_id, round(sum(charge_amount), 2) as amt
from tms.accessorial_event group by shipment_id) a on a.shipment_id = s.shipment_id
select i.shipment_id, i.total_amount as header_total, round(sum(l.billed_amount), 2) as line_total
join settlement.carrier_invoice_line l on l.carrier_invoice_id = i.carrier_invoice_id
select r.shipment_id, r.shipment_number, r.delivered_on, r.rated_cost, i.header_total, i.line_total,
case when i.shipment_id is null then 'missing_carrier_invoice'
left join inv i on i.shipment_id = r.shipment_id
where (i.shipment_id is null and r.delivered_on <= date('2025-12-31', '-35 day'))

derivations.name: dq_rule_result_current
sql: select 'DQ-001' as rule_code, 'carrier invoice PRO does not match any shipment' as issue, cast(c.carrier_invoice_id as text) as entity_id
where not exists (select 1 from tms.shipment s where s.pro_number = c.pro_number)
select 'DQ-002', 'accessorial charge manually overridden without supporting event', cast(accessorial_id as text)
select 'DQ-003', 'TMS billto account missing active xref mapping', cast(b.billto_id as text)
where not exists (select 1 from xref.entity_identifier x
where x.source_system = 'LoadLine TMS' and x.source_record_id = cast(b.billto_id as text)
select 'DQ-004', 'unparseable raw customer extract date', r.customer_id
from raw.oms_customer_extract r where r.source_created_date not like '__/__/____'
select 'DQ-005', 'POD document missing signature', cast(pod_doc_id as text)
from dms.pod_document where signed_by is null
select 'DQ-006', 'duplicate EDI message delivery', cast(e.message_id as text)
where e2.shipment_id = e.shipment_id and e2.message_type = e.message_type
and e2.msg_seq = e.msg_seq and e2.message_id <> e.message_id)
select 'DQ-007', 'EDI message references unknown shipment', cast(e.message_id as text)
and not exists (select 1 from tms.shipment s where s.shipment_id = e.shipment_id)
select 'DQ-008', 'expired xref mapping still flagged active', cast(x.entity_identifier_id as text)
where x.active_flag = 1 and x.valid_to is not null and x.valid_to < '2025-12-31';

imperfections.name: carrier_duplicate_records
imperfections.scenario: Carriers onboarded twice (DBA vs legal name); core.carrier survivorship collapses them and the mdm_stewards queue reviews them.

imperfections.name: edi_late_arrivals
imperfections.scenario: Partner EDI 214 statuses arrive days late; on-time metrics restate.

imperfections.name: edi_orphan_shipment_refs
imperfections.scenario: Carrier PRO never mapped to a shipment; orphan events route to the integration_support queue (DQ-007).

imperfections.name: edi_duplicate_messages
imperfections.scenario: Gateway retries deliver the same message twice (DQ-006).

imperfections.name: accessorial_manual_overrides
imperfections.scenario: Billing analysts add/waive accessorials by hand; DQ-002 flags charges without supporting events.

imperfections.name: raw_customer_date_drift
imperfections.scenario: Legacy extract job pads dates after a patch; staging parses them to NULL (DQ-004).

imperfections.name: lost_customer_xref
imperfections.scenario: Mappings lost in the 2024 integration migration; DQ-003 coverage check catches the TMS side.

imperfections.name: stg_name_survivorship_conflicts
imperfections.scenario: OMS and TMS disagree on customer names after staging survivorship; data_correction work for the stewards.

imperfections.name: carrier_invoice_pro_typos
imperfections.scenario: PRO numbers hand-keyed in the carrier portal; auto-match fails and DQ-001 fires into the settlement_desk queue.

imperfections.name: carrier_invoice_line_restatements
imperfections.scenario: Re-rated invoices land as reversal + restated line pairs; these create the header-vs-line and rated-variance recon breaks.

imperfections.name: pod_missing_signatures
imperfections.scenario: Driver app captures without signatures; DQ-005 feeds the pod_chase queue.

controls.rule_name: Delivered shipments reconcile to carrier invoices
Carrier invoice header equals line sum
POD on file within 5 days of delivery
controls.expected_break_rate: ~6% of delivered (machine residual: POD never received)

dq_rules.condition: no manual override without supporting event

dataflows.name: customer_mastering
dataflows.sequence: oms.customer -> raw extract -> staging -> xref -> core.customer -> wh.dim_customer

dataflows.name: shipment_lifecycle
dataflows.sequence: tms.shipment -> state machine -> tms.shipment_event -> tms.shipment_leg -> wh.fact_shipment -> mart_on_time_performance

dataflows.name: pod_to_billing
dataflows.sequence: delivered -> pod_received (machine) -> dms.pod_document -> pod_chase queue when missing

dataflows.name: carrier_settlement
dataflows.sequence: delivered shipment -> settlement.carrier_invoice/line -> match status -> recon view -> settlement_desk queue
